channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: RFK Jr. says he plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Monday said he plans to tell the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to stop recommending fluoridation in communities nationwide. Kennedy also said he’s assembling a task force to focus on the issue. Also on Monday, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Swollen rivers are flooding towns in the US South after a prolonged deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky on Monday, submerging neighborhoods and threatening a famed bourbon distillery in the state capital.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Bridal shops brace for impact as tariffs affecting wedding dress prices
item.description: Most of the world's wedding dresses are made in China and the tariffs are increasing wholesale prices for dresses and fabrics.
item.dc:creator: Ella Wales, NewsNation

item.title: Texas governor calls November election for vacant US House seat as Democrats criticize timing
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott announced Monday that a special election to fill the late Democratic U.S. Rep. Sylvester Turner's seat won’t be held until November and defended the timing by renewing criticism of past Houston-area elections.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses deadline for return of Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts agreed Monday to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Army reenlists nearly two dozen soldiers ousted for refusing COVID-19 vaccine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army has reenlisted more than 23 soldiers who were discharged for refusing the COVID-19 vaccine, officials said Monday, rushing to implement President Donald Trump's order that troops be rehired and given back pay.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over death of her husband
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Lori Vallow Daybell, who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again Monday. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: SEGAL GOVINDARAO and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida charter captain Brock Horner ordered to have no contact with fisherman after arrest
item.description: A Florida charter captain whose angry exchange with a fisherman went viral has been ordered not to contact the alleged victim, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: ICE agent impersonations spike in wake of immigration crackdown
item.description: Criminal charges have been filed involving people accused of posing as immigration officers and threatening deportation.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Fan-favorite Krispy Kreme item returning for Easter
item.description: Krispy Kreme is celebrating Easter with an all-new doughnut collection, which includes a fan-favorite item. Starting Monday, fans can enjoy the all-new Hoppy Easter Collection, featuring three festive doughnuts and the strawberry doughnut.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump honors Dodgers' 2024 World Series championship team
item.description: President Donald Trump welcomed Shohei Ohtani, Mookie Betts and the reigning champion Los Angeles Dodgers to the White House on Monday to congratulate them for winning the World Series last season. "That is a very good-looking group of people," Trump said as he began his remarks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over husband's death
item.description: Lori Vallow Daybell, the Idaho mother with doomsday religious beliefs who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Miami’s ‘Little Venezuela’ fears Trump's moves against migration
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Wilmer Escaray left Venezuela in 2007 and enrolled at Miami Dade College, opening his first restaurant six years later.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jay North, TV's Dennis the Menace, dies at 73
item.description: North was 6 when he was cast as the smiling troublemaker in the CBS sitcom adaptation of Hank Ketcham's popular comic strip that took place in an idyllic American suburb.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr. says MMR vaccine 'most effective' way to stop spread of measles after Texas child's death
item.description: Kennedy, who has a history of vaccine skepticism, made the remarks after visiting the family of an unvaccinated 8-year-old girl who died from the disease.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) amid President Donald Trump's massive cuts to the Department of Education and his plans to do away with it entirely.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rising rivers threaten US South and Midwest after dayslong torrent of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Kentucky (AP) — Rivers rose and flooding worsened Sunday across the sodden U.S. South and Midwest, threatening communities already badly damaged by days of heavy rain and wind that killed at least 18 people.
item.dc:creator: JON CHERRY, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson strikes deal with Rep. Luna on parental proxy voting
item.description: This marks a development that is set to unlock the House floor after the explosive issue — and the GOP rebellions it sparked — brought the chamber to a standstill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Pres. Nelson announces 15 new temples during April 2025 general conference
item.description: President Russell M. Nelson of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints announced several new temples during the church's April 2025 general conference.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding, tornadoes as storms slash parts of US
item.description: Many of the impacted areas already are heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that spawned deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California town reports third fatality related to rare virus linked to death of Gene Hackman's wife
item.description: Investigators recently blamed the rare virus for the death of Betsy Arakawa, the wife of actor Gene Hackman.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding and tornadoes as storms slash from Texas to Ohio
item.description: DYERSBURG, Tennessee. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding came Saturday for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned deadly tornadoes. Forecasters warned that rivers in some places would continue to rise for days.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and OBED LAMY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tariffs will make sneakers, jeans and almost everything Americans wear cost more, trade groups warn
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sending children back to school in new sneakers, jeans and T-shirts is likely to cost U.S. families significantly more this fall if the bespoke tariffs President Donald Trump put on leading exporters take effect as planned, American industry groups warn.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storms tore up two of America's most iconic trails. Federal cuts have disrupted repairs
item.description: CAMPO, Calif. (AP) — Hiking the Pacific Crest Trail is a challenge, especially for adventurers making the entire run from Southern California to Canada, and Eric Kipperman’s job is to greet them at the start and lay bare the difficulties ahead.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clayton leads Florida to NCAA title game, scoring 34 points in 79-73 victory over SEC rival Auburn
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Walter Clayton Jr. is having an NCAA Tournament for the ages. Florida now just needs him to deliver one more time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Baby boomers are buying more homes than millennials
item.description: Older buyers — who are more likely to purchase homes with cash — have been less affected by elevated mortgage rates.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Al Green says he’ll present articles of impeachment against Trump in next 30 days
item.description: Green, who vowed to bring articles of impeachment against Trump roughly two months ago, argued that Trump does not “deserve the office” he holds.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
